headline: Energy Surges, Software Stumbles as Middle East Chokepoint Fears Set Thursday's Tone
body_html:
Futures open Thursday against a backdrop of escalating Middle East tension after reports of a Saudi tanker ablaze in the Red Sea following Houthi strikes, with oil prices rising for a fifth consecutive session on mounting supply concerns. The U.S. military completed a twelfth straight night of strikes on Iran overnight, while fresh intelligence questions have emerged over possible Russian involvement in Iranian attacks on CIA facilities — a development that threatens to broaden the geopolitical calculus heading into the open.

Wednesday's completed session illustrated the market's positioning clearly: Energy led all sectors with a gain of 1.66%, lifted by natural-gas names EQT Corp and Expand Energy, which rose 8.45% and 5.18% respectively. Wabtec surged 10.04% to top all gainers. On the other side, Software shed 2.10% and Healthcare fell 1.29%, with Pegasystems dropping 16.00% and GE Vernova off 8.69% among the hardest-hit names. Breadth was negative — 221 advancers against 271 decliners in our 501-name universe.

For Thursday, traders should watch the ECB rate decision as the euro approaches a one-week high, monitor any LNG force-majeure extension from QatarEnergy into mid-October, and track whether the Red Sea threat widens beyond tanker incidents. A flood of earnings reports adds another layer of event risk at the bell.

briefs.headline: Red Sea Flares: Houthi Strike Leaves Saudi Tanker Ablaze
briefs.body: Houthi forces claimed responsibility for striking two Saudi tankers in the Red Sea, with Saudi Arabia confirming one vessel caught fire. The attacks open a potential second maritime chokepoint alongside the Strait of Hormuz, raising fresh alarm about global oil and LNG routing as the Iran conflict stretches into its latest phase.

briefs.headline: QatarEnergy Set to Extend LNG Force Majeure Through Mid-October
briefs.body: QatarEnergy is preparing to extend its force-majeure declaration on liquefied natural gas shipments into mid-October, according to reports. The move compounds supply anxiety for European and Asian buyers already scrambling to secure cargoes, and arrives just as buyers are reportedly pressing Qatar and the UAE for cheaper, more flexible long-term LNG contract terms.

briefs.headline: TotalEnergies Posts Best Quarterly Profit in Nearly Three Years
briefs.body: The French energy major reported its strongest earnings in close to three years, crediting elevated oil prices driven by the Iran conflict. The result underscores how integrated majors with upstream exposure are capitalizing on the supply-risk premium currently embedded in crude markets.

briefs.headline: ECB Decision Looms as Euro Approaches One-Week Peak
briefs.body: The euro is trading near its highest level in a week ahead of Thursday's European Central Bank policy decision, drawing close attention from currency and fixed-income desks. The outcome could add volatility to transatlantic capital flows at a moment when geopolitical risk premiums are already elevated across asset classes.

briefs.headline: Congress Clears $1 Trillion Defense Bill Amid Conflict Uncertainty
briefs.body: The U.S. House passed a one-trillion-dollar defense authorization measure despite unresolved questions surrounding the Iran and Israel situations. The legislation clears a key legislative hurdle and may reinforce spending visibility for defense-sector names, though broader strategic concerns about the conflict's trajectory remain open.

briefs.headline: U.S. Military Logs Twelfth Consecutive Night of Iran Strikes
briefs.body: American forces carried out strikes on Iranian targets for the twelfth night running, sustaining an operational tempo that analysts warn is straining available military assets. Separately, reports surfaced that Iranian attacks on CIA facilities have prompted inquiries into a potential Russian advisory role, a development that could meaningfully shift diplomatic dynamics.
